# nanopm

nanopm is an AI-powered product management skill pack for AI coding agents. It automates the full product planning cycle — audit, objectives, strategy, roadmap, PRD, and tickets — directly inside your editor, with persistent memory across sessions.

## What it is

nanopm is a set of skills (slash commands) that run a structured PM workflow inside AI coding agents. Each skill is a markdown file containing instructions for the agent. Skills chain together: each one writes a markdown artifact that the next one reads, so context compounds automatically.

It is not a SaaS tool. It is not a web app. It runs entirely inside your terminal via any compatible AI coding agent.

Works with Claude Code, Mistral Vibe, and OpenAI Codex.

## Who it is for

- Solo founders acting as their own product manager
- Engineers doing product work without a dedicated PM
- Small teams that want a repeatable, structured planning process
- AI-native builders who want product planning next to their code

## What problems it solves

- AI product planning is fragmented: prompts are one-off, context is lost between sessions, outputs don't feed into each other
- Existing tools (Notion, Linear, ChatGPT) don't run a full planning pipeline, don't read your codebase, and don't persist memory across sessions
- nanopm fills the gap between "I have a codebase" and "I have a clear plan"

## How it works

Install with:
```
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/nmrtn/nanopm/main/setup | bash
```

Skills appear as `/pm-*` commands. One command runs the full pipeline:
```
/pm-run
```

The full planning pipeline:
```
interviews → data → audit → objectives → strategy → roadmap → PRD → tickets
```

Each skill also works standalone.

## Skills

**Planning pipeline:**
- `/pm-run` — full pipeline in one command
- `/pm-scan` — read an existing codebase to understand what it does before planning
- `/pm-discovery` — figure out WHAT to build before planning HOW (pre-product / greenfield)
- `/pm-audit` — brutal honest assessment of product, user, and biggest gap
- `/pm-objectives` — OKRs with anti-goals and measurable key results
- `/pm-user-feedback` — aggregate feedback from Dovetail, Productboard, etc; cluster themes
- `/pm-competitors-intel` — monitor competitor pages, diff snapshots, surface strategic implications
- `/pm-strategy` — strategy + mandatory adversarial challenge
- `/pm-roadmap` — outcome-driven roadmap (Shape Up / Scrum / NOW-NEXT-LATER)
- `/pm-prd` — full PRD or Shape Up pitch, adapts to your methodology
- `/pm-breakdown` — break PRD into tasks, create tickets in Linear / GitHub Issues
- `/pm-retro` — compare roadmap vs commits, surface what drifted
- `/pm-upgrade` — upgrade nanopm to the latest version

**Daily ops:**
- `/pm-standup` — morning briefing: what shipped yesterday, today's meetings, top 1-3 priorities
- `/pm-interview` — prepare a user interview guide, or debrief a Granola transcript into FEEDBACK.md
- `/pm-weekly-update` — draft stakeholder update email (CEO, investor, or team), adapted to audience
- `/pm-data` — answer a product question using PostHog or Amplitude (trends, funnels, retention)

## Memory system

Every skill run appends structured entries to `~/.nanopm/memory/{project}.jsonl`. The next skill reads this file to understand what was tried before. Re-running `/pm-audit` six months later gives the agent the full history of prior decisions.

## Key differentiators

- Persistent memory across sessions (no other PM tool does this)
- Lives in your editor — not a separate tab or SaaS tool
- Reads your codebase directly
- Zero-config — works with no integrations (tier 4 always available)
- Adapts to Shape Up, Scrum, Kanban, or no methodology
- Full pipeline: context compounds from audit through to tickets
- Daily ops loop: standup, interview debriefs, and weekly updates feed back into planning

## How it gets data

nanopm tries each tier in order, uses the highest available:
1. MCP — direct tool calls (Linear, Notion, GitHub, PostHog, Google Calendar, Granola, Slack...)
2. API — REST/GraphQL via environment variables
3. Browser — headless scrape
4. Manual — user provides input directly (always works, zero setup)

Connectors: Linear, GitHub, Notion, Dovetail, Productboard, PostHog, Amplitude, Mixpanel, Google Calendar, Granola, Intercom, HubSpot, Jira, Google Drive, Slack.

## Requirements

- One of: Claude Code, Mistral Vibe, or OpenAI Codex
- python3 (standard on macOS/Linux)
- No integrations required

## Repository

https://github.com/nmrtn/nanopm

Built on the SKILL.md standard from gstack (https://github.com/garrytan/gstack).
MIT license.
